mainEntity.name: I already had a bad back before I fell—will they use that to deny my medical bills?

acceptedAnswer.text: Insurance companies often try to deny claims based on pre-existing conditions, but if the work fall made your condition worse (aggravation), it is still legally covered under workers' compensation.
acceptedAnswer.description: A "pre-existing condition" is the #1 tool insurance companies use to deny fall claims. In Utah, the law states that if a workplace accident "aggravates" or "accelerates" a pre-existing condition, it is still a compensable injury. For example, if you already had a minor bulge in your disc, but the fall caused it to rupture, the insurance company is responsible for the treatment. The key is proving the "medical causal link"—showing that the fall made your condition worse than it was the day before. This almost always requires an expert medical opinion. A lawyer can help you obtain a medical report that clearly distinguishes your prior condition from the new trauma.
